criteriaCoverage.evidence: handlers sendBroadcast scopedQuery SELECT 1 FROM broadcasts WHERE id=$1 with ctx.workspaceId before runBroadcast, 404 if not owned; id from params, workspace_id from token never body.
cross-workspace-action.integration: marketer+owner WS-A token -> WS-B broadcast id 404, B status draft, no B outbox; positive control WS-A own -> 200 sent, outbox A=1 B=0. Real PG, 6/6.
add/remove/import-csv call ownsResource(ctx.workspaceId,'segments',id) -> 404 before addManualMembers; test proves WS-B id -> 404 zero rows.

PUT /segments,/campaigns scopedQuery UPDATE (cross-tenant updated=0); GET /profiles/:id scopedQuery 404; broadcasts send guard; admin/:id gated view_all_workspaces + audited. scopedQuery mandatorily prepends workspace_id=$1. No remaining cross-tenant id vector.
after switch to B: 'Beta Loyalty Club' visible AND 'Manual VIPs' count 0 in segment-list testid. Real DOM re-scoping, real Chromium.

recommendations: Add cross-tenant integration cases for DELETE /segments/:id/members and POST import-csv (share the same guard, untested for the vector).

createBroadcast/createCampaign accept template_id/audience_ref/trigger_segment_id from body without verifying ownership; not exploitable (dispatcher loads templates scoped; audience resolved scoped) but validate at creation for defense-in-depth.
Consider composite FK (workspace_id,id) on broadcasts.template_id/trigger_segment_id to make cross-workspace refs structurally impossible.
